content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Barbara Milman 
Chief Consultant 
June 8, 1989 
Assembly Elections Committee 
state Capitol 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Ms. Milman: 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-89-333 
We have received your letter of May 26, 1989 (copy attached) 
seeking written confirmation of the advice I provided in our 
recent telephone conversation. 
This is to confirm that your 
letter accurately reflects that advice. 
If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916) 
322-5901. 
KED/JGM:aa 
Attachment 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
GennCOUnsel 
By: ~1~: G~?!::"~ 
Counsel, Legal Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804..0807 • (916)322 .. 5660 


Dave 
Baker 
May 26, 1989 
John McLean 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 H St., Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear John: 
I am writing to confirm the oral advice you gave me 
concerning an Assemblymember who wishes to advertise a 
secretarial job that is available in his office. 
You advised 
me that, pursuant to regulation 18901, the member may place 
an advertisement in the newspaper, provided that the 
advertisement does not mention the Assemblymember's name or 
indicate in which Assemblymember's office the job is 
available. 
The advertisement may, however, list the 
Assemblymember's office telephone number, but only if the 
number is not identified as being the Assemblymember's 
number. 
I would appreciato written confirmation of this advice. 
Sincerely, 
Barbara Milman 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
June 1, 1989 
Barbara Milman 
Chief Consultant to Assembly Elections Committee 
P.o. Box 942849 
Sacramento, CA 
94249-0001 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-333 
Dear Ms. Milman: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on May 30, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact John McLean an attorney in the Legal Division, 
directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
ruly yours, 
~ th~~4::/~ ~ 
General Counsel 
KED:plh 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

sections.question: An Assemblymember wishes to advertise a secretarial job available in his office. The question is whether, pursuant to regulation 18901, the member may place an advertisement in the newspaper, and if so, what restrictions apply regarding mention of the Assemblymember's name, office identification, and telephone number.
sections.conclusion: Pursuant to regulation 18901, the member may place an advertisement in the newspaper, provided that the advertisement does not mention the Assemblymember's name or indicate in which Assemblymember's office the job is available. The advertisement may, however, list the Assemblymember's office telephone number, but only if the number is not identified as being the Assemblymember's number.
sections.question_synthetic: May an Assemblymember advertise a secretarial job opening in a newspaper, and what restrictions apply to mentioning the Assemblymember's name, office, and telephone number in such an advertisement under regulation 18901?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: An Assemblymember may advertise a secretarial job in a newspaper without mentioning the Assemblymember's name or office identification, but may include the office telephone number if it is not identified as the Assemblymember's number.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains incoming request letter (May 26), acknowledgment letter (June 1), and FPPC response letter (June 8) confirming informal advice. Question and conclusion sections extracted from incoming request letter and FPPC confirmation. High confidence in extraction despite informal nature and multiple letter format.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: An Assemblymember wishes to advertise a secretarial job available in his office. The question is whether, pursuant to regulation 18901, the member may place an advertisement in the newspaper, and if so, what restrictions apply regarding mention of the Assemblymember's name, office identification, and telephone number.

CONCLUSION: Pursuant to regulation 18901, the member may place an advertisement in the newspaper, provided that the advertisement does not mention the Assemblymember's name or indicate in which Assemblymember's office the job is available. The advertisement may, however, list the Assemblymember's office telephone number, but only if the number is not identified as being the Assemblymember's number.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC informal advice letter confirming oral advice regarding restrictions on how an Assemblymember may advertise a secretarial job opening under Political Reform Act regulation 18901.
